articles.title: WHITE HOUSE CRYPTO COUNCIL HEAD BO HINES TO STEP DOWN
articles.summary: Bo Hines, executive director of the White House's crypto council, is stepping down and returning to the private sector. His deputy, Patrick Witt, will take over. Hines, appointed by President Trump in late 2024, met with over 150 crypto leaders during his tenure and will continue advising on AI initiatives under David Sacks.

articles.title: HOW HIGH WILL ETHEREUM GO AFTER BREAKING $4K?
articles.summary: Ethereum surged 24% this week to above $4,330, breaking key resistance around $4,200 in its highest rally since December 2021. Analysts see a Wyckoff Accumulation breakout in play, with a potential move toward $6,000 if the new uptrend holds. A simultaneous breakout from a multi-year symmetrical triangle points to an $8,000 target in the coming months, echoing past multi-month rallies triggered by similar patterns.

articles.title: COINBASE LAUNCHES RETAIL DEX WITH AERODROME
articles.summary: Aerodrome is now fully integrated into Coinbase's frontend, making every Base asset, pool, and launch on Aerodrome instantly accessible to 100M+ Coinbase users. Unlike protocols reliant on proprietary frontends, Aerodrome's design rewards integrations, allowing for deeper liquidity, wider reach, and more rewards for $AERO lockers.

articles.title: WORLD MOBILE LAUNCHES DRONE-BASED, DECENTRALIZED TELECOM PROJECT
articles.summary: World Mobile has partnered with Indonesia's Protelindo to launch a blockchain-enabled 5G network using hydrogen-powered drones in the stratosphere, delivering wireless coverage of up to 15,000 sq km per aircraft. Operating at 60,000 feet with 6ms latency, the system claims to provide connectivity up to 18x cheaper per gigabyte than satellite-based services. The company is targeting a share of the $98.3B sky-based telecom market, positioning itself against competitors like Helium Mobile and Starlink by focusing on areas with higher mobile user density.

articles.title: THE EXHAUSTION OF THE METAGAME: CRYPTO IN ITS POST SLOP ERA
articles.summary: Crypto has entered a “post-slop” era where hype-driven token launches and influencer campaigns no longer drive lasting value—projects must now deliver tangible utility and sustained user activity to earn market trust. The rise of platforms like Kaito exposed the mechanics of paid promotion, but moving forward, only reputation, real products, and sticky liquidity will stand out. Historical cycles—from DeFi Summer to Solana's resurgence—underscore that genuine community engagement and on-chain usage, not endorsements or content volume, fuel long-term success. As quality and transparency become paramount, projects like Hyperliquid that inspire true confidence and put capital at risk are poised to lead the next phase of crypto innovation.

articles.title: LAUNCH BY KINETIQ
articles.summary: Launch by Kinetiq is the first “Exchange-as-a-Service” on Hyperliquid's HIP-3 protocol. It lets any team crowdfund the 1 million+ HYPE stake needed to deploy perpetual futures markets without building backend infrastructure. Launch pools contributor capital into isolated exHYPE tokens per exchange, so slashing events only affect the specific market's staking pool, and fees are distributed transparently to backers. Operators focus on market curation, governance, and community, while HYPE holders earn dynamic yields by choosing which deployments to support. This Shopify-meets-Kickstarter model democratizes perp exchange creation, expands market diversity, and unlocks new yield primitives across the Hyperliquid ecosystem.

articles.title: COINBASE DEX TRADING SHIFTS POWER, SIDESTEPS UNISWAP
articles.summary: As Coinbase rolls out DEX trading to millions of users, Uniswap's frontend fee revenue faces disruption. Unlike Aerodrome, which incentivizes integrations through protocol-based rewards to $AERO lockers, Uniswap Labs profits from its own interface, creating friction with alternative frontends like Coinbase. While Uniswap's moat is built on brand trust, Aerodrome's is built into its smart contracts. The “DeFi mullet” shift, TradFi frontend with DeFi backend, could reroute flows and flip the competitive edge.

articles.title: LINK BUYBACKS SIGNAL NEW ERA FOR CHAINLINK
articles.summary: Chainlink is entering a new phase as token buybacks begin, aligning network success with $LINK value accrual for the first time. Long dubbed a “dino coin,” the project has quietly built one of the deepest moats in crypto, powering mission-critical infrastructure and onboarding top financial institutions under Sergey Nazarov's leadership. With hundreds of millions already in revenue and no regulatory roadblocks for onchain finance, LINK is now positioned as a prime beneficiary of the institutional adoption cycle.

articles.title: PHANTOM LEADS HYPERLIQUID BUILDER REVENUE WITH $1.83M IN ONE MONTH
articles.summary: Since launching Hyperliquid perps directly in-wallet, Phantom has onboarded 25,000+ wallets and generated $1.83M in revenue, outpacing all other builders.

articles.title: LINEA & SBET TO COLLABORATE
articles.summary: Joseph Lubin says Linea and its group of Consensys-linked projects like SBET will soon hit full stride, unlocking capabilities other L2s can't easily replicate.

articles.title: HARVARD REVEALS $116 MILLION INVESTMENT IN BITCOIN
articles.summary: Harvard's latest regulatory filings show the university has $116 million in BlackRock's IBIT Bitcoin ETF, making it and Brown two leading universities with crypto holdings.
